rules.name: 1.1.1 Enable 'aaa new-model'
rules.description: This command enables the AAA access control system.

rules.name: 1.1.2 Enable 'aaa authentication login'
rules.description: Sets authentication, authorization and accounting (AAA) authentication at login.

rules.name: 1.1.3 Enable 'aaa authentication enable default'
rules.description: Authenticates users who access privileged EXEC mode when they use the enable command.

rules.name: 1.1.4 (Exc-Alt) Set 'login authentication for 'line vty'
rules.description: Authenticates users who access the router or switch remotely through the VTY port.

rules.name: 1.1.5 (Exc-Skp) Set 'login authentication for 'ip http'
rules.description: Authenticates users who access the router or switch using HTTP service.

rules.name: 1.1.6 Set 'aaa accounting' to log all privileged use commands using 'commands 15'
rules.description: Runs accounting for all commands at the specified privilege level.

rules.name: *1.1.7 Set 'aaa accounting connection'*
rules.description: Provides information about all outbound connections made from the network access server.

rules.name: 1.1.8 Set 'aaa accounting exec'
rules.description: Runs accounting for the EXEC shell session.

rules.name: 1.1.9 Set 'aaa accounting network'
rules.description: Runs accounting for all network-related service requests.

rules.name: *1.1.10 Set 'aaa accounting system'*
rules.description: Performs accounting for all system-level events not associated with users, such as reloads.

rules.name: 1.2.1  Set 'privilege 1' for local users
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 1.2.2 Set 'transport input ssh' for 'line vty' connections
rules.description: Selects the Secure Shell (SSH) protocol.

rules.name: 1.2.3 Set 'no exec' for 'line aux 0'
rules.description: The 'no exec' command restricts a line to outgoing connections only.

rules.name: 1.2.4 Create 'access-list' for use with 'line vty'
rules.description: Access lists control the transmission of packets on an interface, control Virtual Terminal Line (VTY) access, and restrict the contents of routing updates. The Cisco IOS software stops checking the extended access list after a match occurs.

rules.name: 1.2.5 Set 'access-class' for 'line vty'
rules.description: The 'access-class' setting restricts incoming and outgoing connections between a particular vty (into a Cisco device) and the networking devices associated with addresses in an access list.

rules.name: 1.2.6 Set 'exec-timeout' to less than or equal to 10 minutes for 'line aux 0'
rules.description: CIS benchmark recommends 10 mins, but set it to 0 min 1 sec and check for that time.

rules.name: 1.2.7 Set 'exec-timeout' to less than or equal to 10 minutes 'line console 0'
rules.description: CIS Benchmark recommends exec-timeout of 10 minutes or less on line CON 0

rules.name: 1.2.8 (Exc-Alt) Set 'exec-timeout' to less than or equal to 10 minutes 'line vty'
rules.description: CIS Benchmark recommends exec-timeout of 10 minutes or less on vty lines

rules.name: 1.2.9 Set 'transport input none' for 'line aux 0'
rules.description: When you want to allow only an outgoing connection on a line, use the no exec command.

rules.name: 1.2.10 (Exc-Alt) Set 'http secure-server' limit
rules.description: Exception: limit not needed HTTP is disabled

rules.name: 1.2.11 (Exc-Alt) Set 'exec-timeout' to less than or equal to 10 min on 'ip http'
rules.description: Exception: Exec-timeout not needed HTTP server is disabled

rules.name: 1.3.1 (Exc-Alt) Set the 'banner-text' for 'banner exec'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 1.3.2 (Exc-Alt)  Set the 'banner-text' for 'banner login'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 1.3.4 (Exc-Alt) Set the 'banner-text' for 'webauth banner'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 1.4.1 Set 'password' for 'enable secret'
rules.description: The enable secret command provides better security by storing the enable secret password using a nonreversible cryptographic function. The added layer of security encryption provides is useful in environments where the password crosses the network or is stored on a TFTP server.

rules.name: 1.4.2 Enable 'service password-encryption'
rules.description: When password encryption is enabled, the encrypted form of the passwords is displayed when a more system:running-config command is entered.

rules.name: 1.4.3 Set 'username secret' for all local users
rules.description: The username secret command provides an additional layer of security over the username password. It also provides better security by encrypting the password using non reversible MD5 encryption and storing the encrypted text. The added layer of MD5 encryption is useful in environments in which the password crosses the network or is stored on a TFTP server.

rules.name: 1.5.1 (Exc-Alt) Set 'no snmp-server' to disable SNMP when unused
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 1.5.2 Unset 'private' for 'snmp-server community'
rules.description: An SNMP community string permits read-only access to all objects.

rules.name: 1.5.3 Unset 'public' for 'snmp-server community'
rules.description: An SNMP community string permits read-only access to all objects.

rules.name: 1.5.4 (Exc-Alt) Do not set 'RW' for any 'snmp-server community'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 1.5.5 (Exc-Alt) Set the ACL for each 'snmp-server community'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 1.5.6 Create an 'access-list' for use with SNMP
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 1.5.7 Set 'snmp-server host' when using SNMP
rules.description: Update:

rules.name: 1.5.8 Set 'snmp-server enable traps snmp'
rules.description: SNMP notifications can be sent as traps to authorized management systems.

rules.name: 1.5.9 Set 'priv' for each 'snmp-server group' using SNMPv3
rules.description: Specifies authentication of a packet with encryption when using SNMPv3

rules.name: 1.5.10 (Exc-Manual) Require 'aes 128' as minimum for 'snmp-server user' when using SNMPv3
rules.description: Unable to look up the snmp users  from the configuration. Specify the use of a minimum of 128-bit AES algorithm for encryption when using SNMPv3.

rules.name: 1.6.1 Configure Login Block
rules.description: Update:

rules.name: 1.6.2 (Exc-Skp) AutoSecure
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 1.6.3 (Exc-Skp) Configuring Kerberos
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 1.6.4 (Exc-Skp) Configure Web interface
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.1.1.1.1 Set the 'hostname'
rules.description: The hostname is used in prompts and default configuration filenames.

rules.name: 2.1.1.1.2 Set the 'ip domain-name'
rules.description: Define a default domain name that the Cisco IOS software uses to complete unqualified hostnames

rules.name: 2.1.1.1.3 (Exc-Manual) Set 'modulus' to greater than or equal to 2048 for 'crypto key generate rsa'.  Cannot check with 'show run' command.
rules.description: Exception: Update:

value: ^Minimum expected Diffie Hellman key size : 2048 bits

rules.name: 2.1.1.1.4 (Exce-Alt) Set 'seconds' for 'ip ssh timeout'
rules.description: Exception: Update:

rules.name: 2.1.1.1.5 (Exc-Alt) Set maximimum value for 'ip ssh authentication-retries'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.1.1.2 Set version 2 for 'ip ssh version'
rules.description: Specify the version of Secure Shell (SSH) to be run on a router

rules.name: * 2.1.2 (Exc-Skp) Set 'no cdp run' *
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.1.3 Set 'no ip bootp server'
rules.description: Disable the Bootstrap Protocol (BOOTP) service on your routing device.

rules.name: 2.1.4 (Exc-Skp) Set 'no service' dhcp
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.1.5 Set 'no ip identd'
rules.description: Disable the identification (identd) server.

rules.name: 2.1.6 Set 'service tcp-keepalives-in'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.1.7 Set 'service tcp-keepalives-out'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.1.8 Set 'no service pad'
rules.description: Disable X.25 Packet Assembler/Disassembler (PAD) service.

rules.name: * 2.2.1 Set 'logging-enable' *
rules.description: Enable logging of system messages.

rules.name: 2.2.2 Set 'buffer size' for 'logging buffered'
rules.description: Enable system message logging to a local buffer.

rules.name: * 2.2.3 Set 'logging console critical' *
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.2.4  Set IP address for 'logging host'
rules.description: Log system messages and debug output to a remote host.

rules.name: 2.2.5 (Exc-Skp) Set 'logging trap informational'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.2.6 Set 'service timestamps debug datetime'
rules.description: Configure the system to apply a time stamp to debugging messages or system logging messages

rules.name: 2.2.7 Set 'logging source interface'
rules.description: Specify the source IPv4 or IPv6 address of system logging packets

rules.name: 2.2.8 Set 'login success/failure logging'
rules.description: Without generating audit records that are specific to the security and mission needs of the organization, it would be difficult to establish, correlate, and investigate the events relating to an incident or identify those responsible for one.

rules.name: ** OLD** 2.3.1.1 (Exc-Skp) Require Encryption Keys for NTP
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.3.1.1 (Exc-Skp) Set 'ntp authenticate'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.3.1.2 (Exc-Skp) Set 'ntp authentication-key'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.3.1.3 (Exc-Skp) Set the 'ntp trusted-key'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.3.1.4 (Exc-Skp) Set 'key' for each 'ntp server'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 2.3.2 Set 'ip address' for 'ntp server'
rules.description: Use this command if you want to allow the system to synchronize the system software clock with the specified NTP server.

rules.name: 2.4.1a (Exc-Alt) Create a single 'interface loopback'
rules.description: Configure a single loopback interface.  Some L2-only devices do not have loopbacks

rules.name: 2.4.1b (Exc-Alt) Create a single 'interface vlan'
rules.description: Configure a single vlan interface.  Some L2-only devices do not have loopbacks

rules.name: 2.4.2  Set AAA 'source-interface' to Loopback interface
rules.description: Force AAA to use the IP address of a specified interface for all outgoing AAA packets

rules.name: 2.4.3 Set 'ntp source' to Loopback Interface
rules.description: Use a particular source address in Network Time Protocol (NTP) packets.  Need to allow other interfaces as source.

rules.name: 2.4.4 Set 'ip tftp source-interface' to the Loopback Interface
rules.description: Specify the IP address of an interface as the source address for TFTP connections

rules.name: 3.1.1 Set 'no ip source-route'
rules.description: Disable the handling of IP datagrams with source routing header options.

rules.name: 3.1.2 Set 'no ip proxy-arp'
rules.description: Disable proxy ARP on all interfaces.

rules.name: 3.1.3 Set 'no interface tunnel'
rules.description: Verify no tunnel interfaces are defined.

rules.name: 3.1.4 (Exc-Skp) Set 'ip verify unicast source reachable-via'
rules.description: Exception:

value: ip verify unicast source reachable-via rx

rules.name: 3.2.1 Set 'ip access-list extended' to Forbid Private Source Addresses from External Networks
rules.description: Update:

rules.name: 3.2.2 Set inbound 'ip access-group' on the External Interface
rules.description: This command places the router in access-list configuration mode, where you must define the denied or permitted access conditions by using the deny and permit commands.

rules.name: 3.3.1.1 (Exc-Alt) Set 'key chain'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 3.3.1.2 (Exc-Skp) Set 'key'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 3.3.1.3 (Exc-Skp) Set 'key-string'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 3.3.1.4 (Exc-Skp) Set 'address-family ipv4 autonomous-system'
rules.description: Configure the EIGRP address family.

rules.name: 3.3.1.5 (Exc-Skp) Set 'af-interface default'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 3.3.1.6 (Exc-Skp) Set 'authentication key-chain'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 3.3.1.7 (Exc-Skp) Set 'authentication mode md5'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 3.3.1.8 (Exc-Skp) Set 'ip authentication key-chain eigrp'
rules.description: Specify the type of authentication used in Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP) packets per interface.

rules.name: 3.3.1.9 (Exc-Skp) Set 'ip authentication mode eigrp'
rules.description: Exception:

rules.name: 3.3.2.1 (Exc-Skp) Set 'authentication message-digest' for OSPF area (Manual)
rules.description: Enable MD5 authentication for OSPF.

rules.name: 3.3.2.2 (Exc-Skp) Set 'ip ospf message-digest-key md5' (Manual)
rules.description: Enable Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) Message Digest 5 (MD5) authentication.

rules.name: 3.3.3.1 (Exc-Skp) Set 'key chain'
rules.description: Define an authentication key chain to enable authentication for RIPv2 routing protocols.

rules.name: 3.3.3.2 (Exc-Skp) Set 'key'
rules.description: Configure an authentication key on a key chain.

rules.name: 3.3.3.3 (Exc-Skp) Set 'key-string'
rules.description: Configure the authentication string for a key.

rules.name: 3.3.3.4 (Exc-Skp) Set 'ip rip authentication key-chain'
rules.description: Enable authentication for Routing Information Protocol (RIP) Version 2 packets and to specify the set of keys that can be used on an interface.

rules.name: 3.3.3.5 (Exc-Skp) Set 'ip rip authentication mode' to 'md5'
rules.description: Configure the Interface with the RIPv2 key chain.

rules.name: 3.3.4.1 (Exc-Skp) Set 'neighbor password
rules.description: Enable message digest5 (MD5) authentication on a TCP connection between two BGP peers.

name: CIS Cisco IOS 17.x Benchmark - v2.0.0
description: This Audit Policy is created based on CIS Cisco IOS 17.x Benchmark - v2.0.0
